items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Sonnet 5
items.why_it_matters: Anthropic has launched its next-generation model, Claude Sonnet 5. As your primary coding partner, this model will write cleaner Next.js and Supabase code with fewer hallucinations, significantly speeding up your build times. Try this week: Open Claude Code, update it to use Sonnet 5, and ask it to refactor one of your AI-ingest API routes to see how much faster it resolves bugs.

items.title: Claude Code pricing increases fivefold
items.why_it_matters: A new analysis shows Claude Code's background token usage has quietly made it up to 5x more expensive to run during active development sessions. Since you use Claude Code daily, you need to manage your billing closely to avoid burning through your budget. Try this week: Go to your Anthropic developer console, set a strict daily spend limit, and practice committing smaller, more frequent chunks of code to keep context windows small.

items.title: Free privacy policy generator built specifically for AI apps
items.why_it_matters: To ship AI-ingest to real users, you need a privacy policy that clearly discloses how you handle LLM data and API keys. This tool generates a compliant policy in seconds, removing a common administrative blocker to launching. Try this week: Run your AI-ingest details through this generator, copy the markdown, and have Claude Code quickly scaffold a new `/privacy` route in your Next.js app.

items.title: VPSMaxxing: Run Claude Code and Codex on a virtual private server
items.why_it_matters: This repository provides a setup to migrate your terminal-based AI agents like Claude Code and Codex to a VPS. This keeps your local machine fast and lets your AI agents run long-running code generation or indexing tasks in the background without interruption. Try this week: Spin up a cheap VPS instance, clone this repo, and run a test Claude Code session on it to see if it improves your local development workflow.
